mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined by the unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, triggers recession fears that force equity deleveraging and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and residential mortgage debt outstanding signals a synchronized pullback in household balance sheets and consumption demand, which mechanically reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined index of HOUST and RSAFS declines but SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than half of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial capacity, a sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects robust underlying demand absorbing supply bottlenecks, leading to higher profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases while SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or the rolling correlation between INDPRO momentum and forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly growth in real GDP provides a continuous baseline of corporate earnings expansion that supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the immediate 5-trading-day window following each data release.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns average negative values when GDPC1 growth is positive, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation crosses the 3.5% annual threshold, markets price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which elevates the equity risk premium and suppresses forward valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or the forward return differential between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending power and corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, housing starts act as a leading indicator of broader consumer confidence and durable goods demand; a monthly increase in HOUST signals resilient household balance sheets, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases but SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of observations during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the predictive power of HOUST reverses sign when GDP growth turns negative.
